description: 33 - 15-09-2025 - Emails between Caroline Hurndall (Principal Private Secretary to the Foreign Secretary, FCDO), Ailsa Terry (Private Secretary for Foreign Affairs, No.10), and others

participants.name: Emails between Caroline Hurndall
participants.role: Principal Private Secretary to the Foreign Secretary

text:
Foreign Secretary, FCDO), Ailsa Terry (Private Secretary for Foreign Affairs, No.10), and
others

From: Caroline Hurndall Personal
Sent: Mon 15/09/2025 5:57:28 PM (UTC)
To: "Ailsa Terry (No.10)" Personal JCs
Personal Simon Madden
Personal
ce Tom Bramley Personal , "Symes, Elkie - CO (OFF-
SEN)" Personal , lan Collard *
Personal . Ellen Atkinson
Personal Sean Cherry
Personal
Subject: RE: URGENT - Reply to FAC re. Mandelson CO/UKSV input

OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE

Thanks Ailsa - JCS has tidied up / proofed and this will go to the FS for
clearance tonight. Assuming she’ s happy, we’ 11 send first thing tomorrow

morning. Mr Doughty will also draw on it for his S024 speech (on which there
is a separate chain).

Thanks

Caroline

Caroline Hurndall | PPS/FS | Personal

The Foreign Secretary’ s box deadline is midday, Monday to Thursday. Please
allow a minimum of 48hrs to process work sent to FSPO. Please also copy

Jcs to all submissions/updates to the Foreign Secretary.
OFFICIAL SENSITIVE

From: Ailsa Terry

Sent: 15 September 2025 17:54

To: Jcs ; Simon Madden ; Caroline Hurndall

Cc: Tom Bramley ; Symes, Elkie —- CO (OFF-SEN) ; Ian Collard * ; Ellen Atkinson ;
Sean Cherry

Subject: RE: URGENT - Reply to FAC re. Mandelson CO/UKSV input

OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE
Hi all
I have added in tracked changes including a few typos - this can now go
after a thorough proof read.
I have suggested removing the reference to due diligence as open source
material ref as a) I am not sure we need to add it in and b) it will make it
harder to mount a defence to not release as per the separate advice we
received and are considering.
Ailsa

OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE

From: JCS Personal
Sent: 15 September 2025 14:22

88

To: Simon Madden 
 Ailsa Terry 
Cc: Caroline Hurndall 
; Tom Bramley 
; Symes, Elkie - CO (OFF-SEN) 
 Ian Collard * 
 Ellen Atkinson 
Sean Cherry 
Subject: RE: URGENT - Reply to FAC re. Mandelson CO/UKSV input
OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE
Hi All,
Many thanks for your comments. Please see attached an updated version of the 
letter, this takes onboard your comments and also final checks within FCDO.
Ailsa – on your text for answer 4 to explain how we have handled:
•
Line on mitigations removed as if we aren’t talking about detail we
don’t need to reference mitigations.
•
Line on no ministerial role included in the cover letter.
Grateful if I can have final confirmation you are content (in particular 
No.10 and Cab Sec) and we will then put to the FS.
We are still working to get the letter out asap today, so grateful for quick 
responses (the next FS window to review will be 1500, after that it will be 
1600).
Kind regards,
 | Deputy Private Secretary to the Permanent Under-
Secretaries, Sir Oliver Robbins and Nick Dyer | Permanent Under-
Secretaries’ Joint Private Office
 | King Charles Street | Foreign, Commonwealth & Development 
Office

Office:
On Mon, 15 Sep 2025 at 13:13, Ailsa Terry 
 wrote:
OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE
Dear Caroline
We are content with the draft as we saw it although we would like to answer 
question 4 with something closer to ‘There are strict confidentiality 
rules around sensitive personal information in the vetting process and as a 
result we do not comment on individual National Security Vetting cases and 
any mitigations employed around specific individuals. These processes are 
conducted entirely independently of Ministers and findings/details other 
than the outcome are not shared with them’.
But to ensure we are clearing the final doc, can you resend to this chain 
given there were further edits being made this morning?
Many thanks,
Ailsa
OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE
From: Caroline Hurndall
Sent: 14 September 2025 21:08
To: Tom Bramley 
; Symes, Elkie - CO (OFF-
SEN)
Cc: Simon Madden 
 Ailsa Terry 
; 
; Ian 
Collard * 
Subject: RE: URGENT - Reply to FAC re. Mandelson CO/UKSV input
OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE
Thanks both. I’ll need to make amendments tomorrow once I’ve got them all 
in. So if CabSec wants to see a version, suggest you show him the version I 
sent but with either comments of tracks in as you see fit.
Caroline
Caroline Hurndall │PPS/FS │ 
The Foreign Secretary’s box deadline is midday, Monday to Thursday. Please 
allow a minimum of 48hrs to process work sent to FSPO. Please also copy 
to all submissions/updates to the Foreign Secretary.

To: Elkie Symes 
Cc: Simon Madden 
 Caroline Hurndall 
; Ailsa Terry (No.10) 
; 
; Ian Collard * 
Subject: Re: URGENT - Reply to FAC re. Mandelson CO/UKSV input
Thanks for sharing and sorry to chip in on this too.
On National Security Vetting, I think it is worth being clear that while 
UKSV conduct the vetting checks and make a recommendation, vetting 
decisions are made by the sponsor department.
Would suggest the following addition to the cover letter (and then repeated 
im the questions):
"Separately from this there is a long-standing formal process for National 
Security Vetting which is undertaken independently by the UK Security 
Vetting Agency (hosted by the Cabinet Office) on behalf of individual 
departments and reporting back to them for decision."
Tom
Tom Bramley
Director, Security Policy and Strategy
as
On Sun, 14 Sept 2025, 20:26 Elkie Symes, 
 wrote:
Hi Caroline - Cab Sec still keen to see a version of this that we are all 
content with. If you're able to make Simon's amends happy to put that 
version to him in the morning.
Best,
Elkie
Elkie Symes
Principal Private Secretary to the Cabinet Secretary
70 Whitehall, London, SW1A 2AS
On Sun, 14 Sept 2025 at 20:19, Simon Madden 
 wrote:
Hi Caroline,
Just to follow up on initial response from me.
The references in the draft letter are ok but I’d prefer it to say that 
No10 asked us to do this. We don’t usually play a part in diplomatic 
Personal

appointments.
On the Annex as I said just now it would be wrong to link our due 
diligence with security or vetting. So when you say FCDO didn ’t play any 
part and no security concerns were raised, we would not expect any 
security concerns to be flagged as that was not the due diligence we were 
doing nor are we qualified to make security judgements. So if those 
references in the annex could be amended they would be great.
As I said, we don’t want to give a misleading impression of our due dil 
exercise.
Thanks
Simon
Simon Madden
Director of Propriety & Ethics
| 70 Whitehall | London | SW1A 2AS
Office: 
On Sun, 14 Sep 2025 at 20:04, Simon Madden 
 wrote:
Hi I have a problem. I cannot agree to a reference to our DD process 
being linked to raising or not raising security concerns. That was not 
its purpose. That woujd be misleading.
Our due diligence was on Mandelson generally using open source material. 
It was not a security vetting process and it offered no judgements. It 
was not specific to Epstein. This was in addition to usual pre-employment 
checks carried out by others.
The text we had before was what we are content with
Thanks
Simon
Simon Madden
Director of Propriety & Ethics
| 70 Whitehall | London | SW1A 2AS

OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE
Ailsa,
With apologies for the delay, please see attached a draft response to

the FAC letter of 12 Sept, which I’ve also re-attached here for ease.
The FS has seen this but not yet cleared it for release – you’ll see 
there are a couple of areas that she wants triple checked, so that we 
can be totally confident that we are releasing accurate information to 
parliament. If there are suggested changes from No10 or Cabinet Office 
colleagues, please send them back to me so I can incorporate and clear 
with her.
As per my earlier email, you’ll see we’ve refocussed the letter to be 
based on FCDO process, but drawn on the info sent over by PET and 
CabOffice colleagues in terms of process, for which many thanks.
Best wishes and no doubt speak tomorrow,
Caroline
Caroline Hurndall │PPS/FS │

I’d like our side to review asap so we can feedback any changes and get 
those worked in before release this evening.
Ailsa

Office:
On Sun, 14 Sep 2025 at 15:27, Simon Madden 
wrote:
Sorry to come back so late on this. Ellen and I have just discussed the 
reference to our due diligence. We’d like to amend so we lose the last 
sentence referring specifically to Epstein, as our commission was a 
general request for DD not a specific one on PM/JE
So it should read:
##
In this case, No10 also asked the Propriety and Ethics team in the 
Cabinet Office to undertake some due diligence, based on open source 
material, alongside the usual pre-employment checks.
###
We do not want to create the impression that we were specifically 
commissioned by No10 to look at that point. On second reading Ellen 
spotted this potential reading of the text and I agree too.
Grateful for confirmation of the amendment.
Thanks and apologies for the late intervention.
Simon
Simon Madden
Director of Propriety & Ethics
 70 Whitehall | London | SW1A 2AS

Cameron

This appears to be based on a misunderstanding of how the security vetting 
process operates in FCDO. I think Tom and UKSV colleagues have set that out 
for otehrs over the last few days.
Let me see if I can raise the FCO security leads and check if they have been

On Sun, 14 Sept 2025 at 11:03, Elkie Symes 
 wrote:
Copying in pet and the security team in co 
Elkie Symes
Principal Private Secretary to the Cabinet Secretary
70 Whitehall, London, SW1A 2AS
On Sun, 14 Sep 2025 at 10:08, 
> 
wrote:
OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE
--- Resending to correct email ----
Elkie,
Grateful if you can commission CO/UKSV to input into an Annex that will be 
shared with the FAC in response to their attached letter re. Mandelson. Grateful 
if we can have input by 2pm today please as we are aiming for FS to issue 
the letter today.
Given FCDO do not own the security clearance process, the Foreign Secretary 
will not want to take responsibility for responding to the FAC on these matters. 
We therefore propose the FS write a short cover letter and an annex (owned by 
the Cabinet Office) with responses to their questions.
To support your teams, the questions are set out below and I have included 
where we think the FCDO does have agency to answer the questions are they 
refer specifically to FCDO/the FS.
Annex A - Cabinet Office response to the FAC questions
1.
What security concerns were raised by the agencies
undertaking security clearance ahead of Lord Mandelson ’s
appointment? [For CO to answer]
2.
What was the response of the Foreign, Commonwealth and
Personal
Personal
Personal
JCS
Personal
Personal
Personal
JCS
99


Development Office to any such concerns? FCDO advice was not

3. Was it the assessment of the FODO that any such concerns
represented a potential barrier to Lord Mandelson’ s
appointment?

4. Were any conditions imposed on the remit of Lord
Mandelson as Ambassador in order to mitigate any security
concerns? [For CO to answer]

5. Was there a decision made to dismiss security concerns?
Was such a decision taken by the FCDO or by No. 10? If so,
by whom? [For CO to answer]

6. Jf no decision was taken to straightforwardly dismiss
security concerns raised by the relevant agencies, was a
decision made to suspend or alter: (a) the usual security
vetting requirements; or, (b) the usual timeline for vetting
procedures? [For CO to answer]

7. Was It the assessment of the Foreign Secretary that
there was sufficient time between the announcement of Lord
Mandelson’ s appointment in December and the beginning of
Ais tenure on 10 February to carry out full thorough

8. Did the usual vetting process for diplomatic
appointments commence in relation to Lord Mandelson before
Ais appointment was made public? [For CO to answer]

Kind regards,
Jcs

| Deputy Private Secretary to the Permanent
Under-Secretaries, Sir Oliver Robbins and Nick Dyer | Permanent Under-
Secretaries’ Joint Private Office

Personal King Charles Street | Foreign, Commonwealth &

Development Office
